region_of_origin: Andes Mountains, South America (Peru, Bolivia, Ecuador)
food_subgroup: Pseudocereal — Quinoa (Chenopodium quinoa), seed of a non-grass flowering plant, cooked in water

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for cooked quinoa grain as of 2026; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of cooked white quinoa is approximately 185g (about 1 cup cooked), providing roughly 222 kcal, 8.1g protein, and 39.4g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Quinoa, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 168917
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, SR Legacy Foods Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Direct match to USDA SR Legacy FDC 168917 — Quinoa, cooked. All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from this entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Quinoa — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI 53 for cooked white quinoa sourced from University of Sydney GI Database (one published study). GL 9.8 calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per 100g) / 100 = (53 × 18.4) / 100 ≈ 9.8. Available carbohydrate = total carbohydrate 21.3g minus dietary fibre 2.8g = 18.5g; GL rounded to 9.8 consistent with input record.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (7mg/100g), high magnesium (64mg/100g) and potassium (172mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Cooked white quinoa is highly suitable for hypertensive patients: its negligible sodium content (7mg/100g) combined with high magnesium (64mg/100g — among the highest of all grains) and potassium (172mg/100g) actively supports blood pressure regulation via vasodilation and electrolyte balance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (53), moderate GL (9.8 per 100g), complete protein with high PDCAAS (~0.9)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Cooked white quinoa has a low glycemic index of 53 and a glycemic load of approximately 9.8 per 100g; its complete protein content (4.4g/100g, PDCAAS ~0.9 with all 9 essential amino acids) further blunts postprandial glucose response, making it a preferred grain substitute for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.2g/100g), PUFA-dominant fat profile, zero cholesterol, high dietary fibre (2.8g/100g), high magnesium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cooked white quinoa is cardioprotective: it contains only 0.2g saturated fat per 100g with a PUFA-dominant fat profile (polyunsaturated fat ~1.1g), zero cholesterol, 2.8g dietary fibre supporting LDL reduction, and 64mg magnesium per 100g which is associated with reduced cardiovascular risk; it is suitable as a daily staple for cardiovascular disease management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High folate (42mcg/100g), anti-inflammatory magnesium, complete protein, gluten-free
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Cooked white quinoa provides 42mcg folate per 100g (relevant for patients on methotrexate therapy), high anti-inflammatory magnesium, complete protein for muscle preservation, and is gluten-free — suitable for the 10–20% of RA patients with concurrent gluten sensitivity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cooked white quinoa (Chenopodium quinoa) is a pseudocereal grain originating from the Andes Mountains of South America (Peru, Bolivia, Ecuador), where it has been cultivated for at least 5,000 years. It is prepared by simmering dry white quinoa seeds in water until the germ ring separates and the grain becomes soft and fluffy. The cooked grain is light beige in colour with a mild, slightly nutty flavour and a soft yet slightly chewy texture. Per 100g cooked: approximately 120 kcal, 4.4g complete protein (PDCAAS ~0.9 — contains all 9 essential amino acids including lysine, which is deficient in most grains), 1.9g fat (PUFA-dominant, only 0.2g saturated fat), 21.3g carbohydrate, 2.8g dietary fibre, 7mg sodium, 172mg potassium, 64mg magnesium (highest among common grains), 152mg phosphorus, 42mcg folate, and 1.5mg iron. Glycemic index ~53 (low), glycemic load ~9.8 per 100g. Vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, halal. Widely available in Singapore at Cold Storage, NTUC FairPrice, and RedMart. Clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes (low GI, complete protein blunts glucose response), hypertension (negligible sodium, high Mg and K), cardiovascular disease (low saturated fat, PUFA-dominant, high fibre), and rheumatoid arthritis (high folate for methotrexate patients). Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC 168917. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC 168917 (Quinoa, cooked) — an exact match to the food described. GI value of 53 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database with one published study. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are fully populated from this approved primary source.
notes: Sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 168917 — quinoa contains negligible free sugars in its cooked form; confirmed zero per approved source. Cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as quinoa is a plant-based food. Vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero as quinoa is a plant-based food with no B12. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae and vitamin_c_mg and vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 168917. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 168917. Monounsaturated fat (0.53g) and polyunsaturated fat (1.08g) values sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 168917. Water_g (71.6g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 168917. GI glycemic load of 9.8 per 100g calculated as (GI 53 × available carbohydrate ~18.5g) / 100 ≈ 9.8, consistent with input record. Tamil and Hindi local names are not applicable as quinoa is not a traditional South Asian or Singaporean dish; it is an international health food sold in Singapore supermarkets — no established Tamil or Hindi vernacular name exists for this food. SNOMED CT code not identified for cooked quinoa grain as of 2026.
